s2.ptxt: Start up the engine.
Turn the A/C switch on.

Operate the cooler compressor at an engine speed of approximately 1,000 rpm for 5 to 6 minutes to circulate the refrigerant. This causes most of the compressor oil from the various components of the A/C system to collect in the A/C compressor.
Stop the engine.
Recover the refrigerant from the A/C system using a refrigerant recovery unit.
Hold the front of the V-bank cover and raise it to disengage the 2 clips on the front of the cover. Continue to raise the cover to disengage the clip on the rear of the cover and remove the cover.

atten3.ptxt: Attempting to disengage both front and rear clips at the same time may cause the cover to break.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE COOL AIR INTAKE DUCT SEAL (for 2GR-FE)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 7 clips and intake duct seal.
Disconnect the mass air flow meter connector (*1).
Disconnect the purge VSV connector (*2).
Disconnect the 2 purge VSV vacuum hoses (*3).
Disconnect the purge line hose from the clamp (*4).
Disconnect the No. 2 ventilation hose from the air cleaner hose.
Lock the No. 1 air cleaner hose clamp, and then disconnect the No. 1 air cleaner hose from the throttle body.
Remove the 2 bolts and air cleaner cap.
Remove the air cleaner filter element from the air cleaner case.
Disconnect the 3 vacuum hoses.
Disconnect the mass air flow meter connector (*1).
Disconnect the No. 2 ventilation hose (*2).
Disconnect the hose band (*3).
Disconnect the 3 bands, and remove the air cleaner cap sub-assembly.
Remove the 2 bolts, clamp and air cleaner inlet.
Remove the 2 bolts, clamp and air cleaner inlet.
Remove the bolt and No. 1 air cleaner inlet.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT BUMPER ASSEMBLY (w/o Fog Light)

s2.ptxt: Put protective tape around the front bumper assembly.
Using a screwdriver, turn the pin 90 degrees and remove the pin hold clip.

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the RH side and LH side.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 8 screws, 2 clips, 2 radiator grille protectors and bolt.
Disengage the 2 claws and disconnect the front bumper assembly as shown in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the RH side and LH side.

s2.ptxt: Remove the front bumper assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT BUMPER ASSEMBLY (w/ Fog Light)

s2.ptxt: Put protective tape around the front bumper assembly.
Using a screwdriver, turn the pin 90 degrees and remove the pin hold clip.

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the RH side and LH side.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 8 screws, 2 clips, 2 radiator grille protectors and bolt.
Disengage the 2 claws and disconnect the front bumper assembly as shown in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the RH side and LH side.

s2.ptxt: Disconnect each connector, and then remove the front bumper assembly.
Disconnect the horn connector.
Remove the 3 bolts and separate the hood lock assembly from the radiator support upper.
Remove the clamp and separate the hood lock control cable from the radiator support upper.
Remove the 5 bolts and radiator support upper.
Remove the bolt and hood lock support sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT AIR CONDITIONER TUBE AND ACCESSORY

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and disconnect the air conditioning tube assembly from the condenser.
Remove the O-ring from the air conditioning tube assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Seal the openings of the disconnected parts using vinyl tape to prevent entry of moisture and foreign matter.

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and disconnect the cooler refrigerant discharge pipe from the condenser.
Remove the O-ring from the cooler refrigerant discharge pipe.

atten3.ptxt: Seal the openings of the disconnected parts using vinyl tape to prevent entry of moisture and foreign matter.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 4 bolts and condenser.
Using a 14 mm straight hexagon wrench, remove the cap from the modulator.
Using pliers, remove the cooler dryer.
